definitions.text: Of shape: Circular or cylindrical; having a circular cross-section in one direction.
examples: We sat at a round table to make conversation easier.
The ancient Egyptian demonstrated that the Earth is round, not flat.

definitions.text: Circular or cylindrical; having a circular cross-section in one direction.
examples: We sat at a round table to make conversation easier.

definitions.text: Spherical; shaped like a ball; having a circular cross-section in more than one direction.
examples: The ancient Egyptian demonstrated that the Earth is round, not flat.

definitions.text: A circular or spherical object or part of an object.

examples: The prison guards have started their nightly rounds.

definitions.text: A general outburst from a group of people at an event.
examples: The candidate got a round of applause after every sentence or two.
I look round the room quickly to make sure it's neat.
The farmer fed his cow hay all the year round.

definitions.text: To shape something into a curve.
examples: The carpenter rounded the edges of the table.

definitions.text: To become shaped into a curve.
To finish; to complete; to fill out; see also round out.
examples: She rounded out her education with only a single mathematics class.

definitions.text: To speak in a low tone; whisper; speak secretly; take counsel.
To address or speak to in a whisper, utter in a whisper.
